description_long: This helicopter features a classic bubble-style cockpit with large, curved transparent windows providing excellent visibility for the pilot and passengers. The fuselage is a combination of a metallic frame and a streamlined pod, with exposed structural elements toward the tail boom. The main rotor consists of two long, slender blades with yellow tips, while the tail rotor is mounted vertically at the end of a lattice tail boom. The helicopter is supported by two robust, tubular landing skids, each reinforced with crossbars for stability. The overall color scheme is primarily gray and metallic, with some black and yellow accents, and the design suggests a utilitarian, possibly vintage or light utility helicopter suitable for urban observation or transport.
description: A light utility helicopter with a bubble cockpit, exposed tail boom, two-blade main rotor, and sturdy landing skids, finished in gray and metallic tones.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bubble cockpit, main rotor, exposed tail boom, and full length of the landing skids.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded cockpit windows, the width of the landing skids, and the main rotor blades extending outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the cockpit, tail boom, and landing skids, with the main rotor blades visible from a different angle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail boom, tail rotor, and the parallel alignment of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, with yellow tips and a straight, slightly tapered shape. The blades appear to be approximately 4 meters each in length.
attribute_tail_rotor: Present at the end of the tail boom, vertically oriented with two blades, colored gray with a protective frame around the tail section.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, tubular landing skids made of metal, colored gray, with crossbars for reinforcement and stability. The skids are robust and extend the length of the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Large, curved bubble-style cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides, made of clear glass or acrylic, with minimal framing and no visible tint.
